audit_status.source_base: Greek Titus text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact draft reviewed for truth according to reverence, elders, overseer character, Cretan polemic with ethnic-stereotype safeguard.

verses.restored: Paul, servant of the Divine and apostle of Jesus Messiah, according to trust of chosen ones of the Divine and knowledge of truth according to reverence/godliness,
upon hope of age-long life, which the unlying Divine promised before age-long times,
but in its own seasons manifested his word in proclamation, with which I was entrusted according to command of our Savior/Healer Divine,
to Titus, genuine child according to common trust: grace and peace from Divine Father and Messiah Jesus our Savior/Healer.
For this reason I left you in Crete, so that you might set right the things lacking and appoint elders city by city, as I directed you:
if anyone is above reproach, husband of one wife, having trusting children not accused of wastefulness or disorder.
For the overseer must be above reproach as steward of the Divine, not self-willed, not quick-tempered, not given to wine, not violent, not greedy for shameful gain,
but hospitable, loving the beautiful/good, sound-minded, righteous/just, holy, self-controlled,
holding fast the trustworthy word according to the teaching, so that one may be able both to encourage in healthy teaching and to expose those contradicting.
For there are many disorderly ones, empty-talkers and deceivers, especially those from the circumcision,
whom it is necessary to silence, who overturn whole households, teaching what is not necessary for shameful gain.
One of them, their own prophet, said: Cretans are always liars, harmful beasts, idle bellies.
This testimony is sharp; for this reason expose them sharply, so that they may be healthy in the trust,
not paying attention to Judean myths and commandments of humans turning away from the truth.
To the pure all things are pure, but to those defiled and untrusting nothing is pure, but both their mind and conscience are defiled.
They confess to know the Divine, but by works deny, being detestable and disobedient and unfit for every beautiful/good work.

safety_note: Titus 1 uses sharp ethnic and intra-Jewish polemic. Do not universalize the Cretan quotation as an ethnic stereotype, and do not turn 'circumcision' language into anti-Jewish contempt. The local problem is harmful teaching for gain that overturns households.
translation_notes: Leadership is steward-care and character, not domination.
Healthy teaching is tied to beautiful/good work and truth according to reverence.
